description: Baseline captured via canary: projects route 9.8MB/137 modules; also capture the floor on 3 non-work routes (/msp/inventory, /msp/settings, /msp/billing) — recorded in SCRATCHPAD
Document the reusable ticket intercepted-quick-create shape (create-ticket route + @modal/(.)create-ticket slot + buildCreateTicketHref) in SCRATCHPAD
QuickCreateDialog dispatches client/contact/asset/project/service/product via navigation/href instead of statically importing the dialog components
Intercepted route create-client (real route + @modal/(.)create-client slot importing QuickAddClient only)
Intercepted route create-contact (QuickAddContact)
Intercepted route create-asset (QuickAddAsset)
Intercepted route create-project (ProjectQuickAdd)
Intercepted routes create-service (QuickAddService) and create-product (QuickAddProduct)
Header/QuickCreateDialog no longer statically imports any of the 6 heavy dialogs; canary confirms their graphs are gone from the shell chunk (present only under create-<x> routes)
Phase 1 gates + smoke: typecheck exit 0; ESLint guard 0; no dynamic import added; all 6 intercepted dialogs open+submit; ticket create-route still works
Enumerate WORK routes: grep useDrawer/openDrawer/replaceDrawer + cross-feature use* hooks, trace to their /msp route(s). Any route that triggers or renders drawer content = work
Produce the work vs non-work route lists (resolve ambiguous ones like billing empirically); record in SCRATCHPAD with the measured non-work floor from F001

Extract WorkspaceProviders: a component holding the exact cross-feature provider stack (DefaultLayout 461-472) + DrawerOutlet (575), moved verbatim (folds in the projects TicketIntegration/ClientIntegration relocation)

Decide DrawerProvider state placement (OQ1): keep global in DefaultLayout so chrome can trigger, WorkspaceProviders holds cross-feature providers + outlet only; confirm no surviving chrome trigger loses drawer context

SPIKE (gate): mount WorkspaceProviders over one work route group and confirm the mechanism — validate (work) route-group interaction with the existing @modal parallel slot + create-<x> intercepting routes; fall back to per-work-feature layout.tsx if the group misbehaves (OQ2)
Mount WorkspaceProviders over ALL work routes (via the chosen mechanism); every work route renders the full stack + outlet
SPIKE verifies cross-feature drawer navigation still works on a work route: ticket→client→interaction chain + goBack/forward + drawer.historyBack shortcut, context intact

Remove the cross-feature provider stack (461-472) + DrawerOutlet (575) from DefaultLayout; keep only justified globals (DrawerProvider state per OQ1, QuickAddClientProviderWithCallbacks, command palette, chat)
Non-work routes render only shell chrome; canary confirms /msp/inventory + /msp/settings pull zero tickets/projects/scheduling/clients/user-activities cross-feature modules
Final canary: non-work routes shed the floor; work routes unchanged; compiling >=15 mixed routes yields no Zone-Allocation OOM. Results table filled with before/after per route class
Final gates: full typecheck exit 0; ESLint barrel guard 0; no dynamic @alga-psa import in shell/layouts/msp-composition; packageDependencies.test.ts passes
Final runtime smoke: cross-feature drawer navigation intact on work routes (ticket/task/interaction/workitem/client-quickview chains + back/forward); non-work routes have no broken drawer triggers
